steps.title: Recall the cosine ratio
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\cos B = \dfrac{\text{adjacent to } B}{\text{hypotenuse}}$ — the leg next to angle $B$ over the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Plug in the side lengths
steps.content:
From the triangle, the leg adjacent to $B$ is $15$ and the hypotenuse is $25$:
$$\cos B = \dfrac{15}{25} = \dfrac{3}{5}$$

The answer is 15/25,3/5.
